food_subgroup: Pasta dishes — fast food / quick service restaurant

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Filipino-style sweet-spicy fast food spaghetti. FOODON_00002473 (pasta food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Pasta, cooked) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Filipino-style fast food spaghetti with meat sauce.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Filipino-style fast food spaghetti. SNOMED CT does not have a sufficiently specific food concept for this dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One regular serving of Spicy Jolly Spaghetti is approximately 250 g, as typically served at Jollibee fast food restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Spaghetti with meat sauce, fast food — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21299); adapted with reference to Jollibee published nutrition panel and USDA Branded Food entries for Filipino-style spaghetti
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for fast food spaghetti with meat sauce (NDB 21299) used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values adjusted upward for sodium and sugar to reflect the Filipino-style sweet-spicy sauce profile (banana ketchup, hot dog slices, added sugar) consistent with Jollibee published nutrition disclosures and USDA Branded Food entries for comparable Filipino fast food spaghetti products. Confidence is medium due to absence of an exact HPB or ICMR-NIN entry for this specific dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; spaghetti (white, boiled) GI reference value
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee Spicy Jolly Spaghetti. GI of 52 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database reference for white spaghetti (boiled), which is the primary carbohydrate component. The sweet tomato-based sauce with banana ketchup and added sugar may modestly elevate the effective GI; however, the pasta matrix dominates the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 51.25) / 100 ≈ 26.7, using 250 g serving with 20.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1050 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Spicy Jolly Spaghetti due to its high sodium content (~1050 mg per regular serving), which represents approximately 46% of the WHO-recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; portion reduction or avoidance is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~26.7 per serving) and elevated added sugar from sweet tomato sauce
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Spicy Jolly Spaghetti cautiously; the moderate GI of 52 and a glycemic load of approximately 26.7 per regular serving, combined with added sugars from banana ketchup and sweet sauce, can produce a meaningful postprandial glucose rise and portion control is strongly recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium, saturated fat from processed meat (hot dog slices), and trans fat presence
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Spicy Jolly Spaghetti due to its combination of high sodium (~1050 mg per serving), saturated fat from processed pork and hot dog slices, and detectable trans fat, all of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Jolly Spaghetti is a Filipino-style fast food pasta dish served at Jollibee restaurants, including the Bugis Village outlet in Singapore. It is a spicy variant of Jollibee's signature Jolly Spaghetti, which is itself a distinctively Filipino adaptation of Italian-American spaghetti. The dish features boiled white spaghetti noodles topped with a sweet, tangy, and spicy tomato-based meat sauce made with ground pork, sliced hot dogs (frankfurters), banana ketchup, chilli, and a blend of seasonings. It is typically finished with a topping of grated cheese, giving it a characteristic sweet-savoury-spicy flavour profile that differs markedly from Italian or Western spaghetti bolognese. The texture is soft, the sauce is thick and clingy, and the overall taste is sweet-forward with a chilli heat finish. Nutritionally, a 250 g serving provides approximately 370 kcal, 15.5 g protein, 12 g fat, and 51 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is high at approximately 1050 mg per serving, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low-medium boundary) based on the white pasta matrix, but the glycemic load per serving is approximately 26.7, which is moderate-to-high and relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The dish is classified as ultra-processed due to the inclusion of processed meat products and commercial sauce formulations. It is listed as Halal at the Singapore Jollibee outlet. Common allergens include gluten, dairy, and soy.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Jollibee Spicy Spaghetti?
Is Jollibee spaghetti halal in Singapore?
What is the GI of Jollibee spaghetti?
Is Spicy Jolly Spaghetti suitable for diabetics?
How much sodium is in Jollibee spaghetti?
What are the ingredients in Jolly Spaghetti?
Is Jollibee spaghetti sweet or spicy?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for Spicy Jolly Spaghetti. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy fast food spaghetti with meat sauce (NDB 21299) with adjustments for the Filipino-style sweet-spicy sauce profile. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database reference for white spaghetti. Confidence is medium rather than low because the USDA anchor is a well-validated fast food spaghetti entry and the adaptations are conservative and documented.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for fast food spaghetti with meat sauce and is not expected to be a significant contributor in this dish. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA source for this food category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry used as the nutrient anchor. All other -1 fields reflect genuine absence of data in the approved source rather than confirmed zero values. The Spicy Jolly Spaghetti is a limited-time or permanent menu variant of the standard Jolly Spaghetti; the spicy variant adds chilli to the sauce. The Halal tag is sourced from the field survey listing for Jollibee Bugis Village. Multilingual names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are provided as descriptive translations rather than official brand names, as Jollibee Singapore does not publish official local-language menu names. GI estimated from analogous white spaghetti reference; actual GI of this specific product may differ due to sauce sugar content.
